articles.title: Dollar Falls in Hopes of De-escalation of Middle East Hostilities
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) fell by -0.36% on Thursday and held just above Wednesday's 4-week low. Weaker-than-expected US economic news on Thursday weighed on the dollar. Q4 GDP was unexpectedly revised lower, Feb personal income and spending rose less than expected, and weekly jobless claims rose more than expected...

articles.title: USDCAD approaches a key resistance zone ahead of the Canadian jobs data and US-Iran talks
articles.expand: FUNDAMENTAL OVERVIEW USD: The US dollar sold off across the board on Wednesday after Trump announced on Truth Social a two-sided ceasefire agreement for two weeks while the US and Iran negotiate a lasting peace deal. Since then, the price action became more rangebound due to Israeli attacks against Lebanon which the Iranians have been saying was pa...

articles.title: Gold Set For Another Weekly Gain
articles.expand: (RTTNews) - Gold edged lower on Friday but was set for a third weekly gain, buoyed by the two-week U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement and eased concerns over possible Federal Reserve rate hikes this year.

articles.title: Fed: Limited inflation shock, delayed cuts – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank economist Bernd Weidensteiner notes that the Federal Reserve sees itself in a good position despite Iran‑related inflation risks, with officials expecting only a small core inflation impact.

articles.title: US: Energy shock set to lift headline CPI – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank economists expect a sharp acceleration in US CPI for March as higher gasoline prices feed through. They project headline CPI at 0.95% month‑on‑month, the strongest since June 2022, lifting the annual rate back to 3.4%.

articles.title: US CPI inflation set to increase sharply, in first snapshot since Iran war began
articles.expand: The US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) will publish the March Consumer Price Index (CPI) data on Friday.

articles.title: ECB: Four rate hikes still baseline despite ceasefire – Nordea
articles.expand: Nordea’s Jan von Gerich and Tuuli Koivu updated their ECB outlook before the ceasefire news and now expect four 25bp hikes starting in June.

articles.title: USD/CAD Forecast: Bears turn cautious ahead of US-Iran talks, US CPI, Canadian jobs data
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair gains some positive traction on Friday, snapping a four-day losing streak to the 1.3800 neighborhood or over a two-week low set the previous day. Doubts over the durability of the US-Iran ceasefire support the safe-haven US Dollar (USD) and act as a tailwind for spot prices.

articles.title: AUD/CAD Uptrend Resumes, Path to Parity Hinges on Canada Jobs Data
articles.expand: AUD/CAD is back on the front foot this week, with its uptrend reasserting itself as markets price in a widening gap between a hawkish RBA and a defensive BoC. The spotlight now shifts squarely to Canada’s employment report today, which carries outsized importance after a string of weak labor data. A soft print could accelerate […] The post AU...

articles.title: Gold keeps the red below $4,750 amid firmer USD; US-Iran peace talks and US CPI in focus
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) maintains its offered tone below the $4,750 level through the first half of the European session on Friday, albeit it lacks bearish conviction.

articles.title: The $5,000 Gold test: How upcoming US inflation data could reshape Fed expectations
articles.expand: Whether Gold prices climb toward a $5,000 breakout or face a hard rejection partly hinges on the upcoming March Consumer Price Index (CPI) report due on Friday.

articles.title: AUD/JPY remains subdued near 112.50 due to risk-off mood
articles.expand: AUD/JPY edges lower after four days of gains, trading around 112.50 during the European hours on Friday. The currency cross remains subdued as the Australian Dollar (AUD) holds losses following the release of Chinese Consumer Price Index (CPI) data for March.

articles.title: GBP/USD: Upside risk hinges on 1.3480 close – UOB
articles.expand: United Overseas Bank’s (UOB) economists Quek Ser Leang and Lee Sue Ann highlight a still-positive short-term outlook for GBP/USD after recent gains above 1.3450. They see scope for the Pound (GBP) to advance toward 1.3520, but stress that a daily close above 1.3480 is required.

articles.expand: With the two-week US-Iran ceasefire under strain, crude oil sits at a technical crossroads as traders are holding out for further developments to gauge if a brief dip or a longer-term pullback is due.

articles.title: Massive Dos Bocas fire adds refining risk. Mexico’s flagship refinery stays below capacity

Summary: Dos Bocas is Mexico’s flagship new refinery and a core part of Pemex’s energy self-sufficiency push. Nameplate capacity is about 340,000 bpd, but recent runs have been closer to roughly 205,000 bpd,...

articles.title: The Great Banking Exodus: Why Major Banks Are Threatening to Leave Britain Over a Regulatory Crackdown
articles.expand: Major global banks including JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are threatening to scale back or exit UK operations over proposed ring-fencing reforms, putting Britain's post-Brexit ambitions as a global financial center on a collision course with post-crisis consumer protections.

articles.title: European rates: Pricing disconnect persists – BNY
articles.expand: BNY Strategist Geoff Yu argues that European rate markets still discount too many hikes for the European Central Bank (ECB), Bank of England (BoE) and Swiss National Bank (SNB) despite an improvement in global risk sentiment following the U.S.–Iran ceasefire.

articles.expand: US Dollar, AUD/USD and EUR/USD Analysis: The Greenback’s two-month Petrodollar rally takes a massive hit as an unexpected US-Iran ceasefire agreement shatters war-driven demand. Pressure on the US dollar came after President Donald Trump postponed the threat of strikes on Iranian civilian infrastructure for two weeks. The politician described this as a “bilateral ceasefire” conditional upon the reopening of the...

articles.title: EUR/USD: Testeando una Resistencia Sólida tras un Repunte Notable
articles.expand: El EUR/USD recuperó impulso tras la relajación de las tensiones geopolíticas, aunque el mercado sigue pendiente de las actas de la Fed y de los próximos datos clave de inflación en Estados Unidos.

The news weighed on the USD, while stocks pushed higher and yields moved lower, creating a more risk-on backdrop that pressured the pair to the downside. articles.title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Bulls seize control as USD plummets on US-Iran ceasefire
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair prolongs its uptrend for the third consecutive day and rallies to over a two-week top on Wednesday, with bulls now looking to build on the momentum further beyond the 200-day Simple Moving Average (SMA).
